pages.full_text:
Le Dancing, founded by the owner's grandfather in 1926. The last time they had dancing was in the 1950's for workers on the dam.
Simon & Eve had smoked ham salad & chestnuts Trufader - chunks of potatoes with stretchy cheese.
Simon had pear in wine Eve had crème brulée.
pages.notes: Page contains six glued-in illustrations of colourful gloves (decorative, no captions). A postcard or artwork reproduction of 'Le Dancing' is glued in the upper centre with the title 'Le Dancing' in script on the image. The handwritten text below describes the restaurant. 'Trufader' may be a dish name — could also read 'Truffade' (a regional Auvergne potato dish); 'chestnuts' could read 'chestnuts'. The text appears to continue from a preceding page (left edge is cut off in the gutter area). 'chestnuts' — possibly 'chest nuts' meaning chestnuts; also could be 'chesnuts'. 'stretchy cheese' — likely refers to Cantal or similar melted cheese in truffade.

pages.full_text:
Fortunately, we arrived at the same time as a man who had papers showing a reservation - the papers claimed the Accueil opened at 2. So he got the cleaning girl to ring the Accueil man, who came back.
	We finally chose a pitch right by the river, only 2 find 2 enormous dog poohs, which I cleared up. (Later in the day, Charles put his hand in bird droppings .... the saga continues.)
	We swam in the pool. Then we walked into town for supper. The Isle between the campsite / foot ball field & town was flooded in 2003 & trees replanted. Simon remembered the old trees.
	Charles had steak & chips & glaces.
pages.notes: Page number 43 visible in top-right corner. 'Accueil' is the French word for reception/welcome desk at a campsite. A glued-in circular sticker/label for 'BIEN CUIT' (a restaurant or butcher) with an illustration of a bull's head is on the lower left of the page. 'glaces' likely means ice cream (French). 'The Isle' may refer to an area or path name rather than a specific island — could also be a local place name. The reference to flooding in 2003 suggests this entry was written after 2003; likely post-2003 visit. The narrative is a continuation from the left page and likely from preceding pages.
